title: Tests: PaymentAction wire format and ZEC amount parsing
description:
## What
Verify that the new wire format is correct and the parse/format functions handle all edge cases.

## Tests to write/update

### In core/src/messages.rs (update existing test)
payment_message_carries_session_id:
- After change, action must serialize with amount_zatoshi as a JSON integer (not string)
- Assert: json.contains("\"amount_zatoshi\":10000000") -- oracle: 0.1 ZEC = 10,000,000 zatoshi
- Assert: !json.contains("\"amount\":") -- no old field name

Add: payment_action_request_wire_format:
- Create PaymentAction::Request { chain: Chain::Zcash, amount_zatoshi: 100_000 }
- Serialize to JSON
- Assert json == {\"action\":\"request\",\"chain\":\"zcash\",\"amount_zatoshi\":100000}
- Oracle: 0.001 ZEC * 10^8 = 100,000; serde_json serializes u64 as JSON number (no quotes)

Add: payment_action_sent_wire_format:
- Create Sent { chain: Chain::Zcash, tx_hash: "abc".to_string(), amount_zatoshi: 10_000_000 }
- Assert json contains \"amount_zatoshi\":10000000 (not a string)

### In bot/src/dispatcher.rs (update existing test)
deliver_payment_request_produces_payment_request_event:
- Change amount: "42".into() → amount_zatoshi: 4_200_000_000 (42 ZEC)
- Assert BotEvent::PaymentRequest.amount == "42" (still a String in BotEvent, via zatoshi_to_zec_string)
- Oracle: 42 ZEC = 4,200,000,000 zatoshi; zatoshi_to_zec_string(4_200_000_000) == "42"

### In tui/src/event.rs (update existing test)
Update test at ~line 1788 that constructs PaymentAction::Sent:
- Change amount: "0.1".to_string() → amount_zatoshi: 10_000_000

## Defensive programming
- All test vectors derived from external oracle (ZEC unit definition: 1 ZEC = 10^8 zatoshi)
- No test uses the code under test as its oracle
- All tests must fail before the changes in W1 are applied (proving they test the right thing)

## Acceptance
cargo test --workspace passes with all tests. No tests skipped or weakened.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace: all tests pass. The new wire format tests explicitly verify JSON number (not string) for amount_zatoshi.
close_reason: 3 wire-format tests added: integer serialization, no old field, roundtrip preserves value; all workspace tests pass
